name: Strongly interacting massive particle (SIMP) dark matter
summary: Sub-GeV dark-matter relic abundance is set by 3->2 number-changing self-annihilation in a strongly-coupled hidden sector, instead of the standard 2->2 freeze-out. The required cross sections sigma_{3->2} ~ alpha_eff^3 / m_chi^5 are naturally produced by strong dynamics with confinement scale Lambda ~ m_chi. Provides a natural setting for sub-GeV thermal DM and self-interacting DM with sigma/m ~ 1 cm^2/g relevant to small-scale structure puzzles.

addresses_puzzles.note: Targets sub-GeV mass range below WIMP territory; predicts sizable 2->2 self-interactions (sigma/m ~ 0.1-1 cm^2/g) potentially addressing core-cusp and too-big-to-fail problems.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicted sigma/m sits in the cluster-merger-bound region (Bullet Cluster, Harvey et al.); some SIMP parameter space already excluded.
Couplings to SM needed for kinetic equilibrium with the SM bath are typically mediated by a dark photon with kinetic mixing - direct target of NA64/BaBar/Belle II.
Sub-GeV mass range below typical Fermi-LAT sensitivity; constraints from Voyager, AMS at low energies are more relevant.

new_fields: strongly-coupled hidden sector (e.g., dark mesons / dark baryons)
new_scales: dark confinement scale Lambda_dark ~ MeV - GeV
introduces.note: Dark confinement scale, dark coupling, kinetic-mixing portal to SM, hidden-sector fermion content.

history: Hochberg, Kuflik, Volansky, Wacker (2014), 'The SIMP Miracle', Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 171301, arXiv:1402.5143. Active research program through 2010s-2020s targeting sub-GeV thermal DM with low-energy direct-detection (SuperCDMS, SENSEI, DAMIC) and kinetic-mixing portal experiments.

references.citation: Hochberg, Kuflik, Volansky, Wacker, 'Mechanism for thermal relic dark matter of strongly interacting massive particles' (The SIMP Miracle), Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 171301 (2014)
